title: Daily Briefing — May 13
headline: Tech’s still carrying but rotation risk is flashing, flows want clarity not hype.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us — the broad market’s grinding higher with SPY at $738.18, firmly above its 20 MA ($717.91). Fear & Greed is solidly in 'Greed' territory at 65.5, but not extreme, so it's a soft caution flag, not a stop sign. VIX is behaving, trending below its 20-day (17.86 < 18.04), suggesting volatility sellers are in control and nobody’s panicking yet.

Sector rotation is messy: XLK (Tech) still leads but momentum is weakening (RS=115.8, Mom=99.6), while Financials (XLF, $51.58) and Healthcare (XLV, $145.85) are improving but not breaking out. Energy (XLE, $57.57), Materials (XLB, $52.14), and most cyclicals are lagging and flagged 'HIGH' risk. Watch the canary—XLF is still above its 20 MA ($51.86), but only barely, and if Financials break, that's your first sign to run.

Contrarian radar: Retail demand is still piling in, with leaders like NVDA ($220.78) and AAPL ($294.80) approaching stretched MAs. No Smart Money warning triggers today, but flows into lagging sectors mean the market could start rotating defensively without warning. Abundance mindset—don’t chase, rotation windows on the horizon. If bonds (HYG $79.87, TLT $84.99, LQD $108.56) start breaking their 20 MAs, play defensive and reduce risk immediately.
callout_body:
SPY support: $717.91 (20 MA)
NVDA support: $205.90 (20 MA)
MSFT resistance: $418.29 (20 MA)
XLF risk level: $51.86 (20 MA)
Bonds (TLT): $85.92 (20 MA) — warning if
XLK (Tech) slowing, watch $161.75 (20 MA)
Energy XLE: $57.15 (20 MA)
Fear & Greed: 65.5 (Greed, not extreme)
action_items: Watch Financials (XLF $51.58): Reduce equity exposure if daily close < $51.86 (20 MA)
Rotate into Healthcare (XLV $145.85, improving momentum) if flows confirm sector leadership
closing_quote: Patience, react, don’t predict—if it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
